business.headline: Tax regime to e-verify: Income Tax Department's 7 golden rules for ITR filingThe Income Tax Department has issued seven golden rules for filing ITRs for AY 2026-27. The checklist urges early preparation, careful verification and timely e-verification to avoid delays and lost benefits.

TCS adds over 9,200 employees in Q1, logs biggest hiring in over a yearAlongside higher hiring, TCS reported a solid financial performance for the June quarter. Consolidated net profit stood at Rs 13,349 crore, while revenue rose 14% year-on-year to Rs 72,275 crore.

TCS Q1 results: Revenue jumps 14%; IT major declares Rs 12 interim dividendThe company posted double-digit growth in both revenue and profit, helped by steady demand from clients, especially in the banking and financial services sector. It also announced an interim dividend of Rs 12 per share for its shareholders.

Sensex, Nifty close higher despite US-Iran tensions; TCS results in focusBenchmark indices closed higher on Thursday as investors returned to beaten-down stocks after Wednesday's sharp sell-off. The rebound was led by midcaps and realty shares, while Middle East tensions, crude prices and earnings remained in focus.

India's insurance industry is seeing a Gen Z takeover, but there's a twistA report says Gen Z advisors are selling more insurance policies than older peers. Experienced advisors still dominate higher-value life cover and long-term protection sales.

Aviva becomes first foreign insurer to fully own India life businessAviva has agreed to buy Dabur Invest Corp's remaining 26% stake in its India life venture. The deal makes it the first foreign insurer to fully own an Indian life business under the new FDI rules.

Old vs new tax regime: Which one will help you save more tax?Before you click 'Submit' on your ITR, make sure you've chosen the right tax regime. A few minutes spent comparing the old and new regimes could translate into substantial tax savings.

TCS shares fall 2% ahead of Q1 results. Can it revive investor confidence?TCS enters the June-quarter earnings season with investors braced for weak spending and softer margins. Its commentary on AI, demand and deal wins could shape sentiment across the IT sector.

Missiles fly, markets rise: Why Sensex is climbing despite US-Iran escalationFresh US strikes on Iran and Tehran's threat have reignited tensions around the Strait of Hormuz, after attacks on commercial shipping. Yet, on Dalal Street, investors are responding with surprising calm.

Sensex jumps 600 points despite US-Iran tensions: Why is market rising today?As of 9:40 am, the BSE Sensex was trading 516.15 points higher, or 0.67%, at 77,019.75, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 139.90 points, or 0.59%, to trade at 24,021.95.

Markets recover despite US-Iran tensions; IT stocks under pressureAt 9:40 am, the BSE Sensex was up 516.15 points, or 0.67%, at 77,019.75, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 139.90 points, or 0.59%, to 24,021.95.

Sensex, Nifty today: Will markets crash again amid rising US-Iran tension?GIFT Nifty futures were trading at 23,995.5 at around 7:50 am, indicating that the NSE Nifty50 could open above Wednesday's closing level of 23,882.05.

US states may sue to block Paramount-Warner Bros merger next week: ReportSeveral US states are preparing to challenge Paramount's proposed acquisition of Warner Bros. Discovery in court. The move could delay the deal, deepen financial pressure and intensify antitrust scrutiny.

US court approves Musk-regulator settlement over Twitter shares despite 'red flags'A US judge approved the SEC's settlement with Elon Musk over delayed disclosure of Twitter share purchases. The order also raised questions about why the regulator eased its claims and settled through Musk's trust.

New EPFO portal: Here's how it could make PF claims easier and faster for employeesThe upgraded platform, which will be fully implemented this week after the end of the 'blackout period', is designed to make EPFO services faster, smarter and easier to use. Several processes have also been automated.

IMF cuts India's FY27 growth forecast to 6.4%, but expects strong momentum to continueIn its July 2026 World Economic Outlook (WEO) Update, the IMF projected India's economy to grow by 6.4% in FY2026-27, slightly lower than the 6.5% forecast made in April.

Not Mumbai, not Delhi: This city has India's highest household incomeIf you thought Mumbai or Delhi had India's highest-earning households, think again. A new income ranking has placed another city at the top, with both metros trailing behind.

ITR-5 filing begins: Check eligibility, deadlines and common errors before you fileThe wait is over for taxpayers who file ITR-5. With the Excel Utility now available, here's everything you need to know before filing your return.

Dalal Street bleeds as soaring crude and geopolitical worries spook investorsThe S&P BSE Sensex plunged 1,677.12 points, or 2.15%, to close at 76,503.60, while the NSE Nifty50 fell 516.65 points, or 2.12%, to settle at 23,882.05.

Dalal Street bloodbath: Sensex tanks 1,600 points, more pain ahead for investors?As of 2:42 pm, the sell-off on Dalal Street intensified, with the benchmark BSE Sensex plunging 1,766.68 points, or 2.26%, to 76,414.04, while the NSE Nifty50 tumbled 553.35 points, or 2.27%, to 23,845.35 as escalating US-Iran tensions, a sharp spike in crude oil prices above $78 per barrel and weak global cues triggered broad-based selling across sectors.

entertainment.headline: Akshay Kumar worried about Rajesh Sharma after insect bite, shares moving post Actor Akshay Kumar shared an emotional message after his co-star Rajesh Sharma was hospitalised following an insect bite during a film shoot. Rajesh remains under medical observation in Kolkata.

The Vvaan poster is packed with clues as Sidharth-Tamannaah film gets release date The makers of The Vvaan have unveiled a new poster of the upcoming fantasy adventure featuring actors Sidharth Malhotra and Tamannaah Bhatia. The imagery, featuring a bull, a tiger and a warrior, deepens the mystery around its folklore-inspired world.

Welsh singer Bonnie Tyler dies at 75: Family shares emotional statement Bonnie Tyler, the Welsh singer behind Total Eclipse of the Heart, has died at 75 in Portugal. Tributes from her family and public figures marked the end of a career defined by global hits.

Who is Himesh Patel? Meet the Indian-origin actor in Christopher Nolan's The Odyssey Actor Himesh Patel has drawn attention for playing Eurylochus in director Christopher Nolan's The Odyssey, based on Homer's epic poem. Here's a look at the actor's background, education and professional journey.

Writer Farhad Samji on Akshay-Raveena's reaction to 'Welcome' jokes about their past Farhad Samji opens on the Akshay Kumar-Raveena Tandon reunion in Welcome to the Jungle. He reveals that it was written for humour, not nostalgia and adds how self-aware references landed because both actors were willing to laugh at themselves.

Watch: Ranveer Singh's Dhurandhar moves get the party going at Anshula's reception Actor Ranveer Singh danced with his Gunday co-star Arjun Kapoor and other guests at Anshula Kapoor and Rohan Thakkar's reception in Mumbai. The clips from the star-studded evening are going viral online.

Kay Kay Menon's Adarsh Baal Vidyalaya locks release date Prime Video has unveiled the release date of it upcoming series Adarsh Baal Vidyalaya. The comedy-drama follows a failing school's fight for resilience and a second chance.

I, Nobody review: Prithviraj, a nobody, has a plan but the film doesn'tRelease Date:Thu Jul 09, 2026 I, Nobody review: Director Nisam Basheer's I, Nobody, starring Prithviraj Sukumaran, Parvathy Thiruvothu and Vijayaraghavan, is a heist thriller that doubles up as a social commentary. While the premise and its treatment warrant your attention, the story is dragged well beyond its potential.

Satluj row: PIL in Punjab and Haryana High Court seeks restoration of film on Zee5 A PIL in the Punjab and Haryana High Court has sought restoration of Satluj after its removal from Zee5. The plea questions the absence of any disclosed order and raises concerns over free speech and due process.

Hina Khan, Rubina Dilaik question Gaurav-Akanksha's public image; call out facade Hina Khan and Rubina Dilaik questioned Gaurav Khanna and Akanksha Chamola's public appearances after their separation came to light. The duo revisited Akanksha's divorce revelation and questioned their happy couple image.

CM Vijay's Jana Nayagan gets A rating after 7-month standoff with censor board CM Vijay's Jana Nayagan has been cleared with an A certificate by the Cental Board of Film Certification after nearly a seven-month delay. The film is expected to hit theatres in the third week of July.

Huma Qureshi explains why people don't hate nepotism, they hate entitled star kids Huma Qureshi, in an interview, explained how Bollywood's nepotism row is really about entitlement. She said audiences accept privilege, and react only when opportunities are taken for granted.

Go ahead, collect it: Hema Malini felt Dharmendra walking behind her at Padma event Hema Malini said she felt Dharmendra's presence as she received his Padma Vibhushan from President Droupadi Murmu earlier this year. She also said his final message to the family was to stay united and put family first.

Saif Ali Khan was asked to perform a nude scene in Omkara. He regrets saying no Saif Ali Khan recalled Vishal Bhardwaj suggesting he perform Omkara's mirror sequence completely naked. The anecdote highlights the director's visual instincts and a risk Saif says he would take today.

That one ghost scene in Satluj proves why Diljit Dosanjh is at his haunting best In Satluj, one quiet ghost scene says more about fear, justice and conscience than pages of dialogue ever could. It's a haunting masterclass in restraint by Diljit Dosanjh that stays with you long after the film ends.

Jealous husband 'cooks' wife's pet rabbit on ZeeTV show, internet is traumatised A disturbing scene from the TV show Lakshmi Niwas has gone viral after it showed a man seemingly cooking his wife's pet rabbit out of jealousy. The promo has left viewers shocked and traumatised.

The Cycle Of Love trailer: Priyanka Chopra's documentary tracks 6,000-mile journey Actor-producer Priyanka Chopra has unveiled the trailer of The Cycle of Love, a documentary she executive produced. The film follows a 6,000-mile journey for love, spanning 6,000 miles across continents.

OTT watchlist: 5 new shows and films releasing this week across platforms This week's OTT lineup features everything from Shane Nigam's sports drama Balti and Jayaram-Urvashi's Parimala & Co to the crime series The Westies. Here's a look at the latest releases across SonyLIV, ZEE5, Prime Video and JioHotstar, and why they're worth watching.

Wake up or catch up: Saif Ali Khan explains how Dhurandhar changed Indian cinema Saif Ali Khan agrees that Aditya Dhar's Dhurandhar films changed how Indian cinema uses music. He pointed to songs embedded in the narrative and the franchise's strong reach across streaming and theatres.

general news.headline: Gadkari wanted one ethanol victim, we will present six: Protesters on E20 challengeUnion minister Nitin Gadkari dared critics of the E20 rollout to bring him one person whose vehicle had been damaged due to ethanol-blended petrol. Protesters have accepted his challenge and said they will present the minister with not one, but six such car owners. They have sought an appointment with him.

Jaipur girl jumped to death in school. CCTV shows what happened just beforeNine-year-old Amaira died after she jumped from the fourth floor of her school building on November 1, 2025. The police registered a suicide case with the victim's parents alleging their daughter was bullied at school.

F-35 fighter jets for Turkey? What Trump's move could mean for India, IsraelDonald Trump's decision to lift sanctions on Turkey and reopen the door to F-35 sales marks a dramatic reversal of US policy. The move could reshape the strategic balance in the Middle East, unsettle Israel's military edge, and raise fresh questions for India amid Turkey's growing defence partnership with Pakistan.

What unfolded inside Oval Office that convinced Trump to attack Iran againAn Oval Office briefing on attacks in the Strait of Hormuz pushed Donald Trump to abandon the Iran peace deal. The collapse of the ceasefire has widened the dispute and triggered a sharper military response from both sides.

BSNL launches a phone costlier than the iPhone 17. Here's what it doesBSNL has launched a satellite phone in India priced at Rs 1.34 lakh. The phone is designed for areas where mobile networks do not reach and offers satellite-based calling and emergency support.

Semifinals Live: Muchova outlasts Gauff in 3rd set to reach maiden finalWimbledon 2026 women's singles semifinals Live Updates: Coco Gauff has made a comeback against Karolina Muchova by winning the second set. The match will now be decided by playing the third and the final set of the match. Later in the day, Marta Kostyuk and Linda Noskova will fight it out for a maiden Grand Slam semis berth.

Why earthquake-hit Venezuela turned to Naveen Patnaik's ex-aide PandianFormer BJD leader and bureaucrat VK Pandian, who has remained out of the public eye since quitting politics in 2024, made a quiet visit to quake-hit Venezuela. It was on the invitation of interim president Delcy Rodriguez. Why did Venezuela suddenly invite an Odisha leader?
Trump misread Iran. The consequences are now unfoldingTrump and his advisors have failed to recognise that their actions have turned Iran into a more nationalist, confident and powerful country.

I'm a doctor: The medical tests people waste money onMany people believe that more medical tests mean better healthcare. As a doctor, I often see patients spending thousands of rupees on investigations they may not actually need.

Jaipur woman, 23, gets mother killed for job; hitmen paid Rs 7 lakh, used SUVPolice claimed that Ayushi Sharma plotted her mother Neeraj Sharma's murder with the help of relatives and contract attackers, driven by the lure of family property and a government job.

CM Vijay's Jana Nayagan gets A rating after 7-month standoff with censor boardCM Vijay's Jana Nayagan has been cleared with an A certificate by the Cental Board of Film Certification after nearly a seven-month delay. The film is expected to hit theatres in the third week of July.

4th T20I preview: How do Shreyas Iyer's India come back from 76-all-outEngland vs India, 4th T20I preview: India have lost the opportunity to win the 5-match T20I series. With 2 matches to go, can Shreyas Iyer's team find it in themselves to dig them out of the hole?

Wimbledon's viral towels have an Indian connectionThey're draped over shoulders, clutched between points and often tucked into kit bags as prized souvenirs. Wimbledon towels have a story that leads all the way to India.

Stricter H-1B, Green Card rules from August; Indians could face more hurdlesThe Trump administration is preparing rules that could further tighten H-1B, student visa and Green Card processes. If cleared, the changes could raise costs, increase scrutiny and deepen uncertainty from August. The H-1B route is used by Indian professionals.

Indian-origin US doctor who drove family off cliff in Tesla walks free after therapyThree years after driving a Tesla with his wife and two children off a 250-foot cliff in California, Indian-origin doctor Dharmesh Patel has walked free. A judge dismissed attempt-to-murder charges following his successful completion of a court-ordered mental health treatment programme.

How BLA and TTP rattled Pakistan, killing 38 personnel in Balochistan in 4 daysBaloch rebels and TTP militants have killed at least 38 Pakistani security personnel in Balochistan in the last four days. Here's how the incidents of violence unfolded across Balochistan and what it says about Pakistan's worsening security crisis.

Tamil Nadu railway staff's heroic act saves man seconds before speeding trainThe video showed the railway gatekeeper standing on the platform when he noticed a man on the tracks as a train rapidly approached in Tamil Nadu.

El Nino should have weakened the monsoon. Then why is India flooded?After a stalled June, the southwest monsoon has returned with intense rain across India. Scientists say El Nino delayed the season, but climate change is amplifying downpours.

World's first and only once-weekly insulin launched in IndiaNovo Nordisk has launched Awiqli, the world's first once-weekly basal insulin, in India. The therapy is aimed at easing injection anxiety and improving adherence among adults with diabetes.

Bengaluru's second airport to come up in south of city, announces DK ShivakumarKarnataka has moved ahead with plans for Bengaluru's second airport in the city's southern corridor, with the Chief Minister saying that the site will be chosen to limit costs, ensure feasibility and avoid displacing villages.

Watch: IIT Roorkee's own Titanic? Students ferry across flooded campus on plankHeavy rain has left parts of IIT Roorkee waterlogged, leading to an unusual sight. A video shows students and staff crossing flooded campus roads on a wooden plank steered with long sticks, complete with Titanic's My Heart Will Go On playing in the background, adding an unexpected comic touch.

We're taking lessons from you: PM praises Australia's under-16 social media banPM Modi praised Australia's decision to bar children under 16 from major social media platforms, saying the move offers important lessons for India and the world.

A fresh warning from JD Vance amid a sweeping H-1B probe in USAs the Trump administration launches a sweeping probe into alleged fraud within the H-1B visa programme, Vice President JD Vance has issued a fresh warning to what he called "foreign fraudsters", while promising that jobs in the US would go to American workers.

Delhi waterlogged, Surat flooded, landslides in Uttarakhand, Kerala amid heavy rain Live UpdatesTorrential monsoon rain battered several states, triggering floods, landslides, a building collapse and widespread transport disruption. Thousands were evacuated as rescue operations continued, while the Centre reviewed the situation and the IMD warned of more heavy rainfall.

sports.headline: 4th T20I Live: All eyes on Shreyas Iyer as India look to snap losing streak India (IND) vs England (ENG) Live Score 4th T20I: India take on England in the fourth T20I at Bristol with Shreyas Iyer searching for his first win as captain. Trailing in the series after another disappointing batting display, India need a victory to restore confidence and avoid another damaging defeat.

Wimbledon rigged? Djokovic vs Sinner semi-final timing causes major controversy Wimbledon's decision to schedule Novak Djokovic and Jannik Sinner's semi-final as the second match on Centre Court has sparked speculation among fans, with many questioning whether the order of play gives the world No.1 an advantage amid London's soaring temperatures.

Arnav Paparkar's dream run ends after quarterfinal defeat to Jordan Lee Arnav Paparkar's memorable Wimbledon campaign came to an end with a 6-2, 7-5 quarterfinal defeat to USA's Jordan Lee. Despite the loss, the Indian youngster announced himself on the big stage with a landmark run to the last eight.

Portugal set to appoint Ronaldo's favourite coach after World Cup debacle Portugal are set to appoint Jorge Jesus as their new head coach following Roberto Martinez's departure after the FIFA World Cup. The move could see Cristiano Ronaldo reunited with his former manager from Al Nassr.

Semifinals Live: Coco Gauff vs Karolina Muchova goes into tie-break in final set Wimbledon 2026 women's singles semifinals Live Updates: Coco Gauff has made a comeback against Karolina Muchova by winning the second set. The match will now be decided by playing the third and the final set of the match. Later in the day, Marta Kostyuk and Linda Noskova will fight it out for a maiden Grand Slam semis berth.

France vs Morocco quarterfinal match guide: Can the dark horses stop Mbappe's men? FIFA World Cup 2026: Morocco must find a way to cage in-form Kylian Mbappe if they are to think of beating France in the quarterfinal in Boston. Their task is made doubly difficult by the catastrophic loss of midfield anchor Ismael Saibari to a severe hamstring injury.

Morocco star forgets question after fight breaks out in press conference. Video FIFA World Cup: Brahim Diaz was left bemused after an altercation between reporters disrupted Morocco's pre-match press conference. The strange scene briefly overshadowed the team's World Cup quarter-final build-up against France.

Ben Stokes not done yet, jokes as ICC questions ECB over retirement video Ben Stokes reacted with a cheeky post after reports claimed the ICC questioned the ECB over the release of his retirement speech video during the Trent Bridge Test against New Zealand.

Argentine referee for quarterfinal: France coach deflects pre-match drama FIFA World Cup 2026: Didier Deschamps dismissed the row over Argentina's refereeing team before France meet Morocco in Boston. His camp insisted the officials merit trust, while attention stayed on Morocco's growing threat.

Shapoor Zadran laid to rest in Kabul as Afghanistan teammates pay final tribute Former Afghanistan fast bowler Shapoor Zadran was laid to rest in Kabul as teammates, family members and cricket officials paid an emotional final tribute to one of the pioneers of the country's rise in international cricket.

I don't want to rest: R Ashwin's hilarious mimicry of Sanju Samson goes viral R Ashwin mocked Sanju Samson's Zimbabwe omission through a viral roleplay with Vimal Kumar. The joke sharpened his warning that erratic selections can spread insecurity through India's T20 dressing room.

Shreyas Iyer will be one of India's best captains: Sister Shrestha backs brotherShreyas Iyer's sister Shrestha brushed aside criticism following India's record five-match winless run in T20Is. She expressed confidence that her brother would eventually be counted among India's greatest captains.

Ukraine's Marta Kostyuk blasts IOC over easing of Russian Olympic restrictionsWimbledon semifinalist Marta Kostyuk has criticised the IOC for easing restrictions on Russian athletes ahead of the 2028 Olympics. The Ukrainian said the decision was "terrible" and vowed to "beat every single Russian" she faces at the Games.

The next Wimbledon queen? Gauff, Muchova, Kostyuk, Noskova dream big in semisWimbledon 2026: Coco Gauff, Karolina Muchova, Marta Kostyuk and Linda Noskova headline a wide-open Wimbledon 2026 women's semifinal line-up, with all four chasing a maiden title at the All England Club.

Our kids adore you: Jelena Djokovic is all grace in this message to FelixWimbledon 2026: Jelena Djokovic's heartfelt tribute to Félix Auger-Aliassime cuttingly defies the digital vitriol plaguing modern sport, perfectly mirroring her husband's on-court grace after a brutal, historic five-hour quarter-final marathon on Centre Court.

The blessing and curse of VAR: Football's never-ending debateAs the 2026 World Cup intensifies debates over VAR, we look at how football's pursuit of technological perfection has successfully eliminated blatant refereeing errors yet fundamentally compromised the sport's raw, emotional certainty.

Egypt were robbed: New York City Mayor Mamdani's smooth World Cup jibe goes viralNew York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani slipped a viral 'Egypt were robbed' line into a bus service briefing after Egypt's defeat to Argentina in the Round of 16. His remarks amplified the raging row over refereeing and Egypt's reactions to their exit.

No Trump card: FIFA rejects France appeal over star player's yellow before quartersWorld Cup: FIFA rejected France's appeal against Michael Olise's yellow card before the Morocco quarter-final. The ruling leaves their star winger one booking away from a semi-final suspension.

4th T20I preview: How do Shreyas Iyer's India come back from 76-all-outEngland vs India, 4th T20I preview: India have lost the opportunity to win the 5-match T20I series. With 2 matches to go, can Shreyas Iyer's team find it in themselves to dig them out of the hole?

Flawless Alexander Zverev cracks Fritz code to reach maiden semi-finalsAlexander Zverev ended a seven-match losing streak against Taylor Fritz with a commanding straight-sets victory to reach his maiden Wimbledon semi-final. The French Open champion will now face British wildcard Arthur Fery for a place in the final.

FIFA World Cup: Why fans can't keep match balls?Catching a FIFA World Cup match ball may feel like the ultimate souvenir, but fans are expected to return it almost immediately. Here's why FIFA insists on getting every ball back, and how cutting-edge technology inside the official match ball plays a major role.

They were not robbed: Bhaichung Bhutia sides with referee Egypt vs ArgentinaFormer India captain Bhaichung Bhutia believes that the referee took the right calls in Egypt vs Argentina match. Bhaichung says that VAR is not perfect and they are getting questioned for the right reasons.

Who is Arthur Fery? Tennis player whose father owned a football clubArthur Fery's fairytale Wimbledon run continued as the British wildcard stunned ninth seed Flavio Cobolli to reach the semi-finals. This is the biggest victory of the player's career. But who is this new British wonder kid?

Sourav Ganguly inducted into ICC Hall of Fame on 54th birthdayFormer India captain Sourav Ganguly was inducted into the ICC Hall of Fame on his 54th birthday, becoming the 10th Indian men's cricketer to receive the prestigious honour.

Marta Kostyuk savours Centre Court debut, seals maiden semi berthMarta Kostyuk marked her Centre Court debut with a commanding 6-3, 6-2 win over Jasmine Paolini to reach her maiden Wimbledon semi-final, extending her remarkable run this year.

Mohamed Salah denied clear penalty vs Argentina? New angle proves referee rightEgypt feel that they were denied a clear win against Argentina in the Round of 16 in the FIFA World Cup 2026. One of the major inflection points occurred when Mohamed Salah was denied a penalty in stoppage time, a decision that could have changed the result of the game.

Austrian and British GP Review: 2 weekends, 3 races, and 5 key talking pointsFormula One's Austrian and British Grands Prix delivered drama on and off the track, from Mercedes' growing reliability concerns and Ferrari's title hopes to Max Verstappen's uncertain future, Kimi Antonelli's emergence as a fan favourite, and fresh questions over the FIA's handling of race procedures.

England great Tammy Beaumont to retire from international cricket after Lord's TestEngland opener Tammy Beaumont will retire from international cricket after the one-off Test against India at Lord's Cricket Ground, ending a decorated 17-year career.

India to miss Pratika Rawal for historic Lord's Test, Priya Punia named replacementIndia have suffered a major setback ahead of the historic women's Test at Lord's, with opener Pratika Rawal ruled out and Priya Punia named as her replacement after an impressive India A tour of England.

Spit, spat and an X: Why Egypt coach Hossam Hassan is being celebrated and dissedEgypt's football head coach Hossam Hassan is at the centre of a raging debate following his team's defeat against Argentina in the FIFA World Cup. From waving the Palestinian flag and flashing FIFA's anti-racism X sign to allegedly spitting at fans waving an Israeli flag, his actions have earned him both praise and derision.

Quarterfinals Highlights: Fery stuns Cobolli, Zverev reaches maiden semi-finalsWimbledon 2026 quarterfinals Highlights: Alexander Zverev and Arthur Fery reached their maiden Wimbledon semi-finals after beating Flavio Cobolli and Taylor Fritz respectively. Earlier, Linda Noskova and Marta Kostyuk advanced to the semis after beating Elise Mertens and Jasmine Paolini.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
